source.definition: to put or fill something into a container or onto a vehicle

explanation.sourceText: The English word "load" originates from Old English "ladan", meaning to bring or carry a burden, which evolved into the sense of filling or putting a burden onto something.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „load“ provine din engleza veche „ladan“, care însemna a aduce sau a purta o povară, evoluând în sensul de a umple sau a pune o povară pe ceva.

source.additional: This verb is commonly used to describe filling or putting items onto vehicles or containers. It can also be used figuratively, for example, when referring to loading data.

target.definition: a umple sau a pune ceva într-un recipient sau pe un vehicul

explanation.sourceText: Borrowed from Latin "incaricare", it means to load and is related to "încărcătură" (load).
explanation.targetText: Vom lateinischen „incaricare“ entlehnt, bedeutet es beladen und ist verwandt mit „încărcătură“ (Last).

target.additional: Regulary conjugated verb of the 1st conjugation (-a). It is frequently used to describe loading vehicles or uploading data. It can also be used figuratively.

explanation.sourceText: The verb describes filling or putting a load onto something.

examples.sourceText: The truck is about to be loaded.
examples.targetText: Camionul va fi încărcat în curând.

explanation.sourceText: Used for uploading or loading data.

examples.sourceText: I would load the car if I could.
examples.targetText: Aș încărca mașina dacă aș putea.

examples.sourceText: By loading the data, I save time.

learningTips.text: Think of 'load' as putting a heavy load onto a truck—this helps remember the meaning of 'a încărca'.
Remember your phone 'loading' an app—this connects to the idea of 'a încărca' meaning to upload or fill.
Imagine loading a suitcase before a trip to recall 'a încărca' as putting things into a container.
The English word 'load' sounds like 'lode', a rich source—think of 'a încărca' as putting valuable things in.
